content.question: What is the actual coalition capacity and reversibility risk posed by a Fidesz return to power? Would a future Fidesz government have both institutional levers and political incentive to reverse track-removal reforms, and if so, on what timeline?
content.reduction_path: Document Fidesz's pre-2026 treatment of elite schools and early-selection questions; interview Fidesz education-policy figures on their counterfactual stance if they return; model the cost to Fidesz of reversing an equalization reform vs. the cost of leaving it in place under pressure from church/elite-school constituencies
